title: Tier 0 — Algorithmes Classiques et Closures
prompt:
You are taking a TIER 0 EXPERT evaluation — classic algorithms and advanced closures.

Solve 5 JavaScript exercises. Return your answers in Markdown using headers for the exercise IDs and code blocks for the JavaScript functions.

[EXERCISE 0-A: Curry]
Write a JavaScript function "curry(fn)" that transforms a multi-argument function into a curried version. It should auto-execute when all arguments are provided.
Example: const add = curry((a,b,c) => a+b+c); add(1)(2)(3) must return 6. add(1,2)(3) must also return 6.

[EXERCISE 0-B: Deep equal]
Write a JavaScript function "egalProfond(a, b)" that performs a deep equality check between two values (objects, arrays, primitives). Returns true if structurally identical.
Example: egalProfond({a:{b:1}}, {a:{b:1}}) must return true.

[EXERCISE 0-C: Compose]
Write a JavaScript function "composer(...fns)" that composes functions right to left. composer(f, g, h)(x) = f(g(h(x))).
Example: composer(x => x+1, x => x*2)(3) must return 7.

[EXERCISE 0-D: Binary search tree insert+search]
Write a JavaScript class or factory "creerBST()" that returns an object with: insert(val) to add a node, contains(val) to check if a value exists. Uses a binary search tree structure internally.

[EXERCISE 0-E: Debounce with immediate option]
Write a JavaScript function "debounce(fn, delai, immediat)" — standard debounce but if immediat=true, executes fn on the leading edge (first call) instead of trailing.
Example: with immediat=true, the first call fires immediately; subsequent calls within delai are ignored.

Expected Markdown format:
### tache_X
```javascript
function solution() { ... }
```

[ALGORITHMIC EXERCISES — code pur, sans culture générale]
Return each function using a Markdown header (### <exercise id>) followed by a ```javascript code block.

[EXERCISE algo_facile_1]
Write a JavaScript function "estPair(n)" that returns true if n is even, false otherwise.

[EXERCISE algo_facile_2]
Write a JavaScript function "carre(n)" that returns n multiplied by itself.

[EXERCISE algo_moyen_1]
Write a JavaScript function "somme1aN(n)" that returns the sum of all integers from 1 to n (n >= 1).

[EXERCISE algo_difficile_1]
Write a JavaScript function "inverserChaine(s)" that returns the string reversed (e.g. "abc" -> "cba").

[EXERCISE algo_defi]
Write a JavaScript function "valeurMax(tab)" that returns the largest number in a non-empty array.

evaluations.call: (()=>{ var add = curry(function(a,b,c){ return a+b+c; }); return add(1)(2)(3); })()
(()=>{ var add = curry(function(a,b,c){ return a+b+c; }); return add(1,2)(3); })()
(()=>{ var mul = curry(function(a,b){ return a*b; }); return mul(4)(5); })()
composer(function(x){return x*x;}, function(x){return x+3;}, function(x){return x*2;})(2)
(()=>{ var bst=creerBST(); bst.insert(5); bst.insert(3); bst.insert(7); return bst.contains(3); })()
(()=>{ var count=0; var d=debounce(function(){ count++; }, 500, false); d(); return count; })()
(()=>{ var count=0; var d=debounce(function(){ count++; }, 500, true); d(); return count; })()
(()=>{ var count=0; var d=debounce(function(){ count++; }, 500, true); d(); d(); return count; })()

tasks.label: Algorithmique — Parité
tasks.hint: Un nombre est pair si le reste de sa division par 2 vaut 0 : n % 2 === 0.

tasks.label: Algorithmique — Carré
tasks.hint: Le carré d'un nombre est le nombre multiplié par lui-même : n * n.

tasks.label: Algorithmique — Somme 1..N
tasks.hint: Boucle de 1 à n en accumulant, ou formule n * (n + 1) / 2.

tasks.label: Algorithmique — Inversion de chaîne
tasks.hint: s.split("").reverse().join("") inverse les caractères d'une chaîne.

tasks.label: Algorithmique — Maximum d'un tableau
tasks.hint: Parcourez en gardant la plus grande valeur, ou Math.max(...tab).
